channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Iran has new drone ship in Persian Gulf
item.description: Iran has a new ship with the capability of launching drones, according to satellite images reviewed by Maxar Technologies that were shared with The Hill. The new drone ship, called Shahid Bagheri, is anchored offshore of the Iranian naval port of Bandar Abbas in the Persian Gulf, according to Maxar.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Israel eyes Iran nuke sites amid reports Trump mulls moves to block Tehran atomic program
item.description: Israel is once again said to be eyeing strikes on Iran's nuclear program as the incoming Trump administration is reportedly mulling plans on how to come down hard on Iran without prompting a war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands of Syrians celebrate in central Damascus during first Friday prayers since Assad's fall
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — Thousands of Syrians gathered Friday in Damascus' historic main mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since the downfall of former President Bashar Assad, while giant crowds celebrated in the capital's largest square.
item.dc:creator: ALBERT AJI and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Saudi Arabia's plans to host the men's World Cup 2034 will be harmful for the climate, experts say
item.description: Saudi Arabia says that to host the 2034 World Cup in men’s soccer, it will build or renovate 15 stadiums, create a futuristic city and expand airports and public transport in a massive buildout to accommodate millions of athletes, coaches and spectators.
item.dc:creator: Alexa St. John And Jennifer Mcdermott, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bringing hope and joy: superheroes for children with cancer in Kosovo
item.description: As the year draws to a close, a remarkable initiative involving “superheroes” clad in unique costumes brings unexpected joy — not only to the sick children but also, perhaps even more deeply, to their parents.
item.dc:creator: Florent Bajrami And Llazar Semini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Moldova declares a state of emergency over energy as fears of Russian gas shortage loom
item.description: Moldova’s parliament has voted in favor of imposing a state of emergency in the energy sector over fears that Russia could leave the European Union candidate country without sufficient natural gas supplies this winter.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Suspected Chinese spy with business ties to Prince Andrew barred from UK
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A suspected Chinese spy with business ties to Prince Andrew has been barred from the U.K. because of concerns he poses a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: China's military is staying silent on its recent activity around Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's military kept silent Friday on its large deployment of naval and coast guard ships off Taiwan this week, with its top spokesperson quoting an ancient strategist instead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian forces edge closer to a key eastern Ukraine city in intense fighting, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Fighting around the key eastern Ukraine city of Pokrovsk is “extremely intense” after a monthslong Russian push, Ukraine’s top military commander said, with analysts estimating Russian forces are now within just a few kilometers (miles) of the city.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Indian teen Gukesh Dommaraju becomes the youngest chess world champion after beating Chinese rival
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Indian teenager Gukesh Dommaraju became the youngest chess world champion Thursday after beating the defending champion Ding Liren of China in the final match of their series in Singapore.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria faces crisis and Gaza heads toward famine, World Food Program official says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The deputy executive director of the U.N. World Food Program has been on whirlwind visits to hotspots in the Middle East and Sudan to assess dire humanitarian situations and escalating demands for food from millions of people trapped or fleeing conflicts.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Funeral for activist turns into call for justice for other missing Syrians
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Hundreds of Syrians marched through central Damascus on Thursday in the funeral procession of a well-known anti-government figure, chanting calls for justice for the activist who was killed in prison during the last days of President Bashar Assad’s rule.
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands attend the funeral of a Taliban minister killed by an Islamic State suicide bomber
item.description: GARDA SERAI, Afghanistan (AP) — Thousands of people attended the funeral on Thursday of a Taliban minister killed in a suicide bombing in Afghanistan's capital, Kabul, the day before that was claimed by the Islamic State group.
item.dc:creator: SAIFULLAH ZAHIR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks businessman Leandro Rizzuto Jr. to be ambassador to the Organization of American States
item.description: President-elect Trump announced Wednesday that he is tapping Leandro Rizzuto Jr., former Conair executive and consul to Bermuda, to be an ambassador for the Organization of American States.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Lawmakers call for intelligence assessment on ending U.S. support for Ukraine
item.description: Lawmakers want the key intelligence chiefs to assess U.S. security risks if America stops providing weapons to Ukraine and Russian wins the war. The lawmakers are pressing for a provision to be added to the National Defense Authorization Act, which the House is expected to vote on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: The world’s largest Christmas tree farm in Oregon sells nearly 1 million trees annually
item.description: Oregon produces more Christmas trees than any other U.S. state, with 4.7 million harvested every year. The state produces 33% of the United States' Christmas trees.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump organization officially launches new Saudi Arabia tower
item.description: Eric Trump, executive vice president of the Trump Organization, officially announced the company’s first project in Saudi Arabia on Wednesday, which consists of a 47 floor building.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Hundreds of tractors block central London streets as farmers protest tax change
item.description: An inheritance tax of up to 20 percent will be applied to British farms in 2026. Hundreds of farmers protested the new policy by blocking the streets of London with tractors Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Navy defeats Houthi attacks in Gulf of Aden for second time in weeks
item.description: U.S. Navy destroyers USS Stockdale (DDG 106) and USS O’Kane (DDG 77) successfully defeated a range of Houthi-launched weapons while transiting the Gulf of Aden for the second time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu vows to challenge 'absurd' corruption charges as he takes the stand in his trial
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promised defiantly on Tuesday to knock down corruption allegations against him as he began testifying in his long-running trial, becoming the first sitting Israeli leader to take the stand as a criminal defendant.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 8 killed in building collapse in Cairo
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — An apartment building collapsed on Tuesday in Egypt’s capital, killing at least eight people, authorities said. The Health Ministry said in a statement that the collapse of the six-story building in Cairo’s western neighborhood of Waili also injured three people, who were hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Syria's unaccounted for chemical weapons 'extremely worrying,' UN official says
item.description: Syrian dictator Bashar al-Assad was finally toppled after 13 years of civil war, having ruled Syria since 2000. The world's attention turns to Assad's unaccounted stockpile of chemical weapons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese military makes massive deployment around Taiwan to send 'very simple' message
item.description: Taiwan is raising concerns about a massive deployment of Chinese naval ships and military planes in the past 24 hours, saying the build-up could eventually lead to war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The US believes journalist Austin Tice is alive after disappearing in Syria in 2012, Biden says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Sunday that the U.S. government believes missing American journalist Austin Tice, who disappeared 12 years ago near the Syrian capital, is alive and that Washington is committed to bringing him home after Bashar Assad’s ouster from power in Damascus.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ousted Syrian leader Assad flees to Moscow after fall of Damascus, Russian state media say
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Ousted Syrian leader Bashar Assad fled to Moscow and received asylum from his longtime ally, Russian media said Sunday, hours after a stunning rebel advance seized control of Damascus and ended his family’s 50 years of iron rule.
item.dc:creator: ABDULRAHMAN SHAHEEN, SARAH EL DEEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Russia abandoned Syria's Assad, never should have been involved
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said on Sunday that Russia's abandonment of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad led to his downfall.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Italian nun among 25 arrested in raid against ‘Ndrangheta mafia
item.description: The nun, 57, is also well-known for her service in Milan's run-down districts. In February she was one of the recipients of the "Golden Panettone", an annual Milanese civic award.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump back on the world stage at Notre Dame reopening in Paris
item.description: Zelenskyy said his first face-to-face encounter with Trump since the election was “good and productive,” adding they had agreed to continue working together.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation
